reasoning: The goal of reaching 95% financial inclusion by 2024 was extremely ambitious and has not been met. Multiple reliable sources reporting on data up to 2024 and 2025 confirm that Nigeria's financial inclusion rate was around 64% to 74% in 2023/2024. For instance, the EFInA report for 2023 indicated a 74% financial inclusion rate, and an academic paper published in October 2025 stated that by 2024, the financial inclusion rate reached 64.1%, failing to hit the CBN's strategic targets. Even under the broadest definition of financial inclusion (which includes non-banking channels), the rate is far below 95%. Since the percentage of adults with an actual bank account is even lower than the overall financial inclusion rate, it is virtually impossible that the threshold of 95% was achieved safely before January 1, 2025.
